outcome_text: Approaches and metrics for measurement of AI risks enumerated during the MAP function—including risks of third-party entities—are identified and documented, and the approaches include: rigorous software testing and performance evaluation approaches; assessment of AI systems for adherence to requirements and trustworthiness characteristics; assessment of whether AI system performance meets legal and regulatory requirements or industry standards; ongoing monitoring and reassessment of AI system performance against organizational risk tolerances.
scoring_text: Skill in designing AI risk measurement approaches and metrics, including software testing and performance evaluation methodology, trustworthiness characteristic assessment, compliance measurement against legal, regulatory, and industry standards, and ongoing performance monitoring against organizational risk tolerances.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing AI risk measurement approaches and metrics, including software testing and performance evaluation methodology, trustworthiness characteristic assessment, compliance measurement against legal, regulatory, and industry standards, and ongoing performance monitoring against organizational risk tolerances.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI conformity assessments under the EU AI Act, including risk classification determination, technical documentation evaluation, and compliance assessment against applicable high-risk AI system requirements.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-1.1 (MEASURE) equal WIDAI RC domain. RC-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.667, cosine: 0.757). Secondary: AG-K-010 (AG, cosine: 0.754), AG-S-005 (AG, cosine: 0.738).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.7/10 (raw 0.667). Shared vocabulary: [against, ai, assessment, compliance, evaluation, risk, skill]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [approaches, characteristic, designing, industry, legal, measurement] vs WIDAI terms [act, applicable, assessments, classification, conducting, conformity] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.900 (P=0.893, R=0.907). Balanced P/R — the 35-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.7%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.757 > STS 0.667 (gap +0.089). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [against, ai, assessment, compliance, evaluation] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 23.3%. Shared tokens: [against, ai, assessment, compliance, evaluation, risk, skill]. NIST AI RMF-only: [approaches, characteristic, designing, industry, legal, measurement]. WIDAI-only: [act, applicable, assessments, classification, conducting, conformity].
